title: Cybernetics
author: Norbert Wiener
content: “Information is information, not matter or energy.”
action: Record: Treat the flow of data as the primary lever for controlling any physical or social system.

title: Introduction to Systems Thinking
author: Donella Meadows
content: “The least obvious part of the system, its function or purpose, is often the most crucial determinant of the system’s behavior.”
action: Record: Identify the hidden objective of a system to predict its inevitable output, regardless of its stated rules.

title: General System Theory
author: Ludwig von Bertalanffy
content: “The organism is not a machine, but a system of parts in dynamic interaction.”
action: Record: Analyze parts only through the lens of their interaction within the larger structure; independent analysis is error-prone.

title: The Fifth Discipline
author: Peter Senge
content: “Systems thinking is a discipline for seeing wholes. It is a framework for seeing interrelationships rather than things.”
action: Record: Map the feedback loops of a situation before acting; circular causality is more common than linear cause-and-effect.

title: Introduction to Cybernetics
author: W. Ross Ashby
action: Record: To successfully manage a complex threat, you must possess at least as much internal complexity (options) as the threat itself.
